title: Let Not Your Heart Be Troubled
subtitle: After Judas goes out, the Lord consoles his own with his farewell at the Supper

stillness: Be still, and let the noise of the world fall away.
Judas has gone out; only the cleansed remain with their Cleanser.
Sit quietly now, as one of his own, and listen.
gospel: Little children, yet a little while I am with you.
The world sees me no longer; but you shall see me.
Let not your heart be troubled.
You believe in God; believe also in me.
In my Father's house are many mansions.
I go to prepare a place for you.
And I will come again, and take you to myself;
that where I am, you also may be.
I will not leave you orphans.
Peace I leave with you, my peace I give to you;
not as the world gives do I give to you.
It is expedient for you that I go;
for if I go, I will send him to you.
behold: After Judas had gone out, the world remained behind with its Cleanser.
And the Lord Jesus, consoling his disciples, made for them a most beautiful sermon,
full of glowing coals of sweetness and of love,
with words most sweet, honeyed with the honey of heaven, and luminous with the light of God.
He calls them Little children, and shows the tenderness of his friendship and his love for them.
For when friends part from one another, they burn most of all with the warmth of love.
consider: He says, Yet a little while I am with you: a little time only, until the seizing and the death.
Let not your heart be troubled at my going by death, for it is for my glorifying.
Here we are taught not to be troubled over our friends who die in the confession of the faith;
for it is the way to their glorifying and to our help,
since our friends, being now with God, can aid us far more than while they lived in the world.
Let not your heart be troubled, I say, because I have said that I shall die,
but be made firm by faith, believing that I shall rise again.
In my Father's house are many mansions: diverse sharings of blessedness,
for according to the differences of merits, there are also distinctions of rewards.
I will not leave you orphans: in this he shows himself a father to them.
Peace I leave you, my peace I give you: not as the world gives, do I give;
for the world gives carnally, I spiritually; the world for a time, I forever; the world outwardly, I within.
It is expedient that I go, that you may know me spiritually, and cease to know me after the flesh,
lest, the Word having been made flesh, you be content as with milk.
For Delicate is the divine consolation, and it is not given to those who admit another.
prayer: O Lord, prepare what you are preparing:
us, that is, for yourself, and yourself for us,
that there may be a multitude of mansions for the diversity of merits:
one more, another less, we shall be sharers of you.
go and come to us, sending the Holy Spirit,
that where you are, we also may be.

latinJewel.latin: Dominus Jesus discipulos consolando, fecit eis sermonem pulcherrimum, ignitis plenum carbonibus dulcedinis et amoris.
latinJewel.english: The Lord Jesus, consoling his disciples, made for them a most beautiful sermon, full of glowing coals of sweetness and of love.
